note: Sprint 5.18b: hardens the human broadcast guard before the new sost-cli is installed in production. C++ subcommand `sost-cli sendrawtransaction <hex>` now validates its own input (empty hex, odd-length hex, non-hex characters, extra positional arguments all rc=1) and handles --help / -h with a specific usage line by deferring those flags to the subcommand handler when a subcommand positional has already been collected; the master print_usage banner now lists sendrawtransaction. Python broadcast guard bumped to v0.2 with an always-on audit trail: every --mode human-broadcast invocation that reaches subprocess writes a receipt to disk, including node_rejected (rc!=0 or timeout), parse_error (stdout had no Txid line), and txid_mismatch (node returned a different txid). Receipt v0.2 schema gains required fields broadcast_attempted, broadcast_result_status (enum dry_run|broadcasted|node_rejected|txid_mismatch|parse_error), node_txid_observed, node_stdout_sha256, node_stderr_sha256 — all schema-strict and additionalProperties:false. New exit codes: 0 success/dry-run, 2 pre-subprocess refusal, 3 subprocess attempted-and-failed. broadcast_performed only stays true on broadcasted; every other state forces it false. Web console renders broadcast_attempted, broadcast_result_status and node_txid_observed; badge distinguishes broadcasted / dry-run / attempt-failed. NO new wire format, NO consensus, NO RPC server changes; only sost-cli.cpp self-validation logic added (~80 lines). Tests: trinity suite green; new test_sost_cli_sendrawtransaction.py skips if the binary is not built.
